behaviors.description: Data from all three sources (metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, deployment log) is included in the content passed to summary generation
behaviors.justification: Multiple modules must work together (API clients, data aggregator) to collect data from three external sources. A Unit test with mocked sources would not catch real data format issues, API authentication failures, pagination problems, or rate-limiting responses that occur with actual APIs.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the three API endpoints with realistic response formats (JSON schemas, status codes, edge cases). Verify the aggregated data passed to summary generation includes fields from all three sources and handles pagination/truncation correctly.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting
behaviors.justification: Graceful degradation requires testing the interaction between multiple modules (data collector, error handler, report builder) when one source fails. A Unit test would not verify that other sources' data is still included and the report completes despite the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Mock one of the three API endpoints to return a failure (HTTP 500, timeout, malformed response). Verify the report is generated with a 'data unavailable' note for that section, the other two sections use valid data, and the process completes without raising an unhandled exception.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data
behaviors.justification: Summary accuracy depends directly on what the LLM produces, including non-determinism and edge cases. Mocking the LLM would always return a logically correct summary, hiding real failures like hallucinations, value omissions, misrepresentations, or mixing up metrics across sections.
behaviors.test_description: Call a real LLM with test data containing specific, verifiable key values (exact metrics numbers, issue counts, dates). Verify the summary includes all key values accurately, doesn't hallucinate additional metrics, and doesn't confuse values between data sections.

behaviors.description: A complete report from data collection through email delivery reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly
behaviors.justification: This multi-step workflow includes an agentic component (LLM summary quality) and real infrastructure (email delivery). A System test would miss LLM issues; an Agentic test would miss email delivery failures. Only a Workflow test covering both the full pipeline and the LLM non-determinism catches failures in data collection, summary generation quality, email formatting, and delivery.
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end test: collect data from all three sources using realistic mocks, generate narrative with a real LLM, format and send the report to a test recipient through a test SMTP server or mock email service. Verify the email arrives with correct headers, encoding, and content matching the input data and summary.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run
behaviors.justification: Testing the full retry cycle requires real filesystem operations (file permissions, disk I/O, concurrent access), scheduler behavior (timing, job re-execution), and email service failure conditions. While a mocked scheduler could work at Integration level, real scheduler behavior (cron timing, process lifecycle, signal handling on retry) requires System-level testing to catch race conditions and state issues.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate email service unavailability (connection refused or timeout). Verify the report is saved to disk with correct format and readable by the retry handler. Trigger or simulate the next scheduled run and verify the saved report is successfully delivered, with no duplicate emails sent.
